pr.title: Cleanup several functions to use inlines in HSD
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Replaced repeated hand-written HSD object reference/deletion logic with existing inline helpers in baselib GObj, JObj, and Shadow code. The PR removed duplicated direct class_info release/destroy calls and a local dec_refcount helper, using hsdDelete and ref_DEC instead.
what_changed: In HSD_GObj_80391120, replaced explicit class_info->release and class_info->destroy calls with hsdDelete(obj) after ref_DEC(obj).
In HSD_JObjUnref, replaced two repeated release/destroy blocks on jobj with hsdDelete(jobj), preserving the surrounding ref_DEC, iref_CNT, jobj_iref_INC, release_child, and iref_DEC control flow.
In HSD_JObjUnrefThis, replaced explicit release/destroy calls with hsdDelete(jobj) when iref_DEC_alt(jobj) succeeds and ref_CNT(jobj) is below zero.
In shadow.c, removed the local inline dec_refcount(u16* ref_count) helper.
In HSD_ShadowRemove, replaced manual camera ref_count decrement logic and direct class_info release/destroy calls with ref_DEC(cobj) followed by hsdDelete(cobj).
smart_moves: Recognized that repeated direct vtable-style release/destroy sequences were equivalent to the existing hsdDelete inline/helper and collapsed them to the canonical abstraction.
Removed a local duplicate refcount decrement helper in shadow.c once the shared ref_DEC helper expressed the same HSD object reference-count operation.
Kept the original higher-level branch structure intact, limiting the cleanup to obvious inline substitutions and reducing matching risk.
Improved consistency across GObj, JObj, and Shadow cleanup paths by routing object deletion through the same helper.
decomp_lessons: When decompiled code shows repeated patterns of parent.class_info->release followed by parent.class_info->destroy, check whether an HSD inline such as hsdDelete already represents that sequence.
Prefer shared HSD reference-count helpers like ref_DEC over local hand-written decrement functions when the object layout and behavior match.
Inlining cleanup can reduce noisy casts to HSD_Class and make object lifecycle code easier to compare across baselib modules.
A cleanup PR can improve future matching and maintainability even when it mostly deletes code, provided the replacement helper captures the same call sequence.
Before introducing or keeping local inline helpers, search adjacent HSD headers/source for canonical macros or inline functions with the same semantics.
naming_conventions: Uses existing HSD helper naming such as hsdDelete for release/destroy deletion semantics.
Uses ref_DEC, ref_CNT, iref_CNT, iref_DEC, iref_DEC_alt, and jobj_iref_INC style names for HSD reference and internal-reference operations.
Preserves existing non-decompiled symbol names such as HSD_GObj_80391120 while improving the function body.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replace explicit class_info->release((HSD_Class*) obj) and class_info->destroy((HSD_Class*) obj) pairs with hsdDelete(obj) when the generated code remains equivalent.
Use ref_DEC(cobj) instead of manually taking &cobj->parent.ref_count and decrementing it through a local helper when the target is an HSD object-compatible refcount.
Retain surrounding null checks and conditional structure where possible to avoid perturbing codegen beyond the inline substitution.
Eliminate duplicate local inline definitions when a shared inline likely produces the intended matching pattern.
follow_up_queries: Find other baselib code that manually calls class_info->release followed by class_info->destroy and may be replaceable with hsdDelete.
Search for local refcount decrement helpers equivalent to ref_DEC in HSD modules.
Audit HSD object lifecycle functions for inconsistent use of ref_DEC, hsdDelete, and direct class_info calls.
Check whether remaining HSD_GObj or HSD_JObj unnamed functions can be clarified through existing inline helpers.
